reviews_data.review_text:
I stopped by to see what was in the strip mall, check out the Fitness place. I saw this little gem and decided to stop in and see what they had.

Brazilian Eats and Treats -

Wow! 🤩 oh my goodness, sweet as well as savory food items.

Can I just tell you the women were gracious and enjoyable to speak with! Very cute, clean, cozy inside.

Amazing selection of desserts, and near the register was where the savory items/ breads are.

I don’t know what I liked best to be honest, but I can’t wait to go back and try some of their lunch plates!

Hands-down, my favorite was this absolutely fabulous coconut dessert in gold foil. Even the woman behind the counter said she ate one almost daily!!

I would be hard pressed to find anything like this anywhere and I have eaten out A LOT!

Anybody who sees my reviews knows my favorite dessert is the Tres Leche at Miro Spanish Grille.

That is still the case - yep, absolute best très Leche anywhere I’ve ever had from New York to here in Charlotte.

However, if you love coconut?
You’ve got to run and try this treat.
I would be surprised to find anything like it anywhere!

There was another smaller coconut treat that you can see in the picture in the takeout container. It is very sweet for me, so I had to eat small bites, but it was really amazing as well! I personally could not eat the whole thing but I ate it over a couple of days after lunch or dinner as a treat. Everyone’s preferences are different though so again this is something I would love to have you try and respond with your impression and opinion.

The big Golden square is cornbread and it’s definitely a different take on southern cornbread, which one would expect of course. I personally loved the texture and the flavor. This is more along the lines of what I look for in cornbread personally.

Please note, that white coconut dessert in the paper above the cornbread?

That’s the super sweet coconut treat and mentioned. I think they make it small for a reason lol! The flavor is very concentrated in a good way.

The donut or what I call the spiral donut? It wasn’t bad at all. It is a very firm texture and dough. I don’t know that I would get it again not because it was bad, just a personal preference and again that beautiful coconut dessert in the cold foil? That’s money for me! 🥹😍

She did say that they love Miros but the Brazilian Tres Leches is different.
It was, but it very good and mine had strawberries in it. A lighter fluffier experience. 🤩

As for what I call stuffed breads/empanadas - very flavorful, fresh, and the handmade dough? Each one had a slightly different texture and taste - excellent. Oh, I will say that I really enjoy the minced ham, great flavor not too salty yet. Wonderful complement of ingredients.

Oh! And what I believe is a stuffed yucca item? It was stuffed with the chicken and the texture and again spot on with ingredients and combination of flavors. So so so good you have to eat it there or at least in the car, 😂 the outside is beautifully, lightly crisp with an amazing flavor and texture. I LOVE yucca, really really enjoyed it and cannot wait to go back.

As this is within a mile of where I live?
I better join the gym next-door!

In any case, really great local spot, everything made homemade handmade in house. Professional gracious service. Worth the patronage and the visit!

Would love to hear what others have to say about their experience and what they bought!

After speaking with the crew my manager remembered you and what happened. 
The youth attendant is North American not a Brazilian native and was having hard time to understand what your husband was asking in Portuguese , not impatient like you mentioned but frustrated because she couldn’t get the order correct and the manager came to rescue and explain to the attendant that it was português he was trying to order . She replied sorry she didn’t realize it was Portuguese and responded back to your husband ..vc quer falar português então falaremos em português.. 
I make sure to have an attendant that speaks English and Portuguese to take care of all clients not just Brazilian clientele. All the clients are important to us. We don’t miss treat anyone and it doesn’t matter what nationality.
Should be reported to our management, for sure would be explained right the way on this terrible matter.
I know what has been said but I am not in control which way you understood and interpreted the situation. Infelizmente foi um erro de comunicação .
I apologize you felt the way at a misunderstanding conversation and language barrier.

reviews_data.review_text:
The food is really good and staff usually is friendly. However, recently one of the ladies was quite rude with my husband - Portuguese is not his first language but he knows a little and likes ordering in Portuguese. The attendant lacking patience  I guess, responded in a rude manner and mouth talked him to a colleague sarcastically saying “ah ele quer falar Portugues, então vamos falar Português”.
This is such a bummer to say the least. This store is sharing a little of the Brazilian culture in another country, you could  have been more friendly to all your customers especially the nationals that host your business here, that even care to learn our language and consume products from back home.
As a Brazilian, I’m ashamed and will not recommend the place anymore considering I’m not sure how my non-Brazilian friends and family will be treated.

We came in with a larger group, none of us knowing anything about Brazilian food.
Lo and the rest of the staff made sure to explain the food and flavors to us as well as make menu recommendations to us. All of which were incredible.
The picahna was some of the best beef I've ever eaten and even the rice was the awesome, too. We also tried a pastel which had a crispy flaky pastry and the chicken and cheese filling was delightful! It was fried perfectly without being greasy and heavy on the stomach. I would also very highly suggest the ham and cheese filled fried pastry (I forgot the name of it) as it was also a favorite.
To top it off, the passion fruit juice and passion fruit cake made me reminisce on my honeymoon, but was also the most heavenly cake I've ever put in my mouth.
Blown away by not only the quality and flavor, but the kindness and service, too!

What a wonderful place for Brazilian pastries, foods, and desserts! We were warmly greeted by the owners of the restaurant, and since it was our first time, they patiently walked us through the menu, specials of the day, food and desserts in the cases, and different drink options.

We ordered a variety of things to try and they were all fantastic! The cakes were decadent but not overly sweet. The savory pastries were fresh and delicious unlike anything we have had before. We will definitely come back to eat a full meal since they have daily specials made in house from their kitchen.

Overall, our experience was top notch. From the service to the food, and even the music and vibe of the restaurant, we could not have asked for much more. You must check this place out for some authentic Brazilian Eats & Treats!

Brazilian eats and treats is exactly what South Charlotte needs. Great local place where you can stop in and get tasty, fresh, homemade food  any time of day. The fresh bread alone should get you in the door.

The food is great. If you are not familiar with Brazilian food, don’t let that scare you. You can find plenty of familiar things, while discovering new items you will grow to love. If you are not sure what something is, just ask someone to walk you through what they have. Don’t miss out on the good stuff simply because you don’t know what it is. No matter what you get, you can’t go wrong. I hope this place is here to stay. I plan on stopping in 3 or 4 times per week!

Good Luck!

reviews_data.review_text:
I stopped by to see what was in the strip mall, check out the Fitness place. I saw this little gem and decided to stop in and see what they had.

Brazilian Eats and Treats -

Wow! 🤩 oh my goodness, sweet as well as savory food items.

Can I just tell you the women were gracious and enjoyable to speak with! Very cute, clean, cozy inside.

Amazing selection of desserts, and near the register was where the savory items/ breads are.

I don’t know what I liked best to be honest, but I can’t wait to go back and try some of their lunch plates!

Hands-down, my favorite was this absolutely fabulous coconut dessert in gold foil. Even the woman behind the counter said she ate one almost daily!!

I would be hard pressed to find anything like this anywhere and I have eaten out A LOT!

Anybody who sees my reviews knows my favorite dessert is the Tres Leche at Miro Spanish Grille.

That is still the case - yep, absolute best très Leche anywhere I’ve ever had from New York to here in Charlotte.

However, if you love coconut?
You’ve got to run and try this treat.
I would be surprised to find anything like it anywhere!

There was another smaller coconut treat that you can see in the picture in the takeout container. It is very sweet for me, so I had to eat small bites, but it was really amazing as well! I personally could not eat the whole thing but I ate it over a couple of days after lunch or dinner as a treat. Everyone’s preferences are different though so again this is something I would love to have you try and respond with your impression and opinion.

The big Golden square is cornbread and it’s definitely a different take on southern cornbread, which one would expect of course. I personally loved the texture and the flavor. This is more along the lines of what I look for in cornbread personally.

Please note, that white coconut dessert in the paper above the cornbread?

That’s the super sweet coconut treat and mentioned. I think they make it small for a reason lol! The flavor is very concentrated in a good way.

The donut or what I call the spiral donut? It wasn’t bad at all. It is a very firm texture and dough. I don’t know that I would get it again not because it was bad, just a personal preference and again that beautiful coconut dessert in the cold foil? That’s money for me! 🥹😍

She did say that they love Miros but the Brazilian Tres Leches is different.
It was, but it very good and mine had strawberries in it. A lighter fluffier experience. 🤩

As for what I call stuffed breads/empanadas - very flavorful, fresh, and the handmade dough? Each one had a slightly different texture and taste - excellent. Oh, I will say that I really enjoy the minced ham, great flavor not too salty yet. Wonderful complement of ingredients.

Oh! And what I believe is a stuffed yucca item? It was stuffed with the chicken and the texture and again spot on with ingredients and combination of flavors. So so so good you have to eat it there or at least in the car, 😂 the outside is beautifully, lightly crisp with an amazing flavor and texture. I LOVE yucca, really really enjoyed it and cannot wait to go back.

As this is within a mile of where I live?
I better join the gym next-door!

In any case, really great local spot, everything made homemade handmade in house. Professional gracious service. Worth the patronage and the visit!

Would love to hear what others have to say about their experience and what they bought!

What a wonderful place for Brazilian pastries, foods, and desserts! We were warmly greeted by the owners of the restaurant, and since it was our first time, they patiently walked us through the menu, specials of the day, food and desserts in the cases, and different drink options.

We ordered a variety of things to try and they were all fantastic! The cakes were decadent but not overly sweet. The savory pastries were fresh and delicious unlike anything we have had before. We will definitely come back to eat a full meal since they have daily specials made in house from their kitchen.

Overall, our experience was top notch. From the service to the food, and even the music and vibe of the restaurant, we could not have asked for much more. You must check this place out for some authentic Brazilian Eats & Treats!

The desserts were absolutely amazing and everything we tried was delectable. The chicken pie and other meat dishes were heavenly and made us crave for more. The owners were extremely kind and friendly, and the staff were also very sweet and welcoming, which created a warm and pleasant atmosphere.

The energy of the place was beautiful, and it was clear that the owners and staff put a lot of care and attention into their work. Overall, I was thoroughly impressed with the quality of the food and the atmosphere of the place. I will definitely be recommending this place to all my friends and family, as I believe that what they are doing there is truly magical. Thank you, Brazilian Eats & Treats, for an amazing  experience.🤍

Brazilian eats and treats is exactly what South Charlotte needs. Great local place where you can stop in and get tasty, fresh, homemade food  any time of day. The fresh bread alone should get you in the door.

The food is great. If you are not familiar with Brazilian food, don’t let that scare you. You can find plenty of familiar things, while discovering new items you will grow to love. If you are not sure what something is, just ask someone to walk you through what they have. Don’t miss out on the good stuff simply because you don’t know what it is. No matter what you get, you can’t go wrong. I hope this place is here to stay. I plan on stopping in 3 or 4 times per week!

Good Luck!
